inline_comments.message:
shouldn't the specific logback sit in their respective charts?
Also, let's have one configmap.yaml

I see that in more than 1 place, you've deviated from the standard we tried to set for the image tag.
Please stick to:
"{{ .Values.global.repository | default .Values.repository }}/{{ .Values.image }}"

I know that when using docker hub there some magic that has to happen because you don't specify a repo.  I figured this out when doing the mysql chart for APPC.

Please use this in values.yaml
repository: registry.hub.docker.com
image: library/IMAGE-NAME:IMAGE-VERSION

We should be able to host a copy of any of these docker hub images in a local repo and not be forced to use docker hub.

this is an init container image, so we need to differentiate between the app image and this one.
how about:
{{ .Values.init.repository }}/{{ .Values.init.image }}?

I think you are right about the logback,
in so log configmap is separated, do you think we should also join them?

take a look at mike's example in the SO chart.
He used something like:
image: "{{ .Values.global.loggingRepository }}/{{ .Values.global.loggingImage }}"

image: "{{ .Values.global.repository | default .Values.repository }}/{{ .Values.image }}"
Why flip all of these to true?  Shouldn't it just be aai?

There is another issue to solve here.
The service name used to be aai-service and was fixed.  Many other apps that want to talk to aai used the KubeDNS entry "aai-service.namespace".  Now the service is named "release-aai" after the chart name and the communication via service is broken.  

We either rename the chart from aai to aai-service or find and update all references to aai-service.namespace and prefix it with the release and drop the word service to align with the chart name.
other ideas are welcome but I think they should stick to some sort of standard and not be 1 off band-aid fixes.

This should be:
repository: nexus3.onap.org:10001
image: aaionap/haproxy:1.1.0
I couldn't find these parameters being used anywhere.  Is there a reason why you added them?

Sorry...my bad.  should be:
repository: registry.hub.docker.com
image: aaionap/hbase:1.2.0

sudo docker pull nexus3.onap.org:10001/aaionap/haproxy:1.1.0
Pulling repository nexus3.onap.org:10001/aaionap/haproxy
Error: image aaionap/haproxy:1.1.0 not found

sudo docker pull registry.hub.docker.com/aaionap/haproxy:1.1.0
1.1.0: Pulling from aaionap/haproxy

yes, but this image is not logging, nor the main pod image.
I think it is best to add under init
also, the image is not available in the repo you listed, but is in the docker.io repo

my bad..I should have been clearer.  I meant to use the example from SO as a guide not as what we should use in this AAI chart :)
What I was hoping to convey is using something like this:
image: "{{ .Values.global.dockerhubRepository }}/{{ .Values.global.busyboxImage }}"
Should be ONAP Active and Available Inventory

Please create a parameter in values.yaml:
.Values.config.keyStorePassword

Please create a parameter in values.yaml:
.Values.config.keyStorePassword
missing {}. Is it needed at all?
I didn't see this port in deployment or services
Need to use loggingrepository. Otherwise when deploying from onap (and if there is override of global.repository) the container won't find the image.
This is problematic, because repository is not onap.
This will create too large port. wrong parameter.
should be in a separate file named {{ include "common.fullname" . }}
This is not the right parameter.

There should be another parameter. This one should have the value of internalPort.
Example:
  type: NodePort
  name: log-kibana
  externalPort: 5601
  internalPort: 5601
  nodePort: 53
Why is it under aai/resources? To which chart is it related?
Like Mandeep suggested, use something like {{ .Values.global.dockerhubRepository }}
no need to touch this... however after rebase it will be fixed automatically.
we override with the subchart value. this is a preparation for when all images are hosted in one repo

why?
this allows the use of a future hosted repo for all images +
for now the value is taken from the subchart, and replaces the global
since there are other mappings to that name, decided to leave as filebeat
it is related to both resources and traversal

better under service.
Also, for it to make any effect, I think it needs to be a service...
Since hbase is not in nexus3.onap.org, a different repository parameter should be used.

Better be a parameter.
Need to make sure that all places that point to aai service have the right value.
it is not defined as a service
the name is shared between subcharts and also {{ include "common.fullname" . }}-log is used for logbacks in each chart
